structure.title: Preface
structure.summary: The partial document is a USDA Rural Development “Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program” income limits table for FY 2024. It shows how guaranteed housing program income eligibility varies by state, county, and metropolitan area, with Alabama displayed in this excerpt. For each listed area, the table provides adjusted income limits by household size from 1 to 8 persons for three categories: Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income (Guaranteed Loan). It also includes footnotes explaining how limits are calculated for households larger than 8 and how moderate income is defined.

structure.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
structure.summary: The partial document contains only an instruction asking the reader to directly return a description of the document’s main points and not include any other text.

nodes.title: STATE:ALABAMA
nodes.summary: This partial document is an appendix from HUD Form HB-1-3555 listing FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the Guaranteed Housing Program in Alabama. It presents income thresholds by area, including MSAs, HUD Metro FMR areas, and counties, with categories for Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income-Guaranteed Loan. The table breaks limits down by household size from 1 to 8 persons, with a note that households above 8 add 8% of the 4-person limit per additional person. It also defines how moderate income is calculated and shows multiple pages of county-by-county income limit tables across Alabama.

nodes.title: STATE:ALASKA
nodes.summary:
This excerpt is a table of FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the USDA Rural Housing Service guaranteed housing program. It lists income thresholds by household size (1 to 8 persons) for multiple areas in Alaska and Arizona.

Main points covered:
- State-by-state income limit schedules for guaranteed housing programs.
- Separate categories for very low income, low income, and moderate income guaranteed loan limits.
- Different limits for Alaska areas with a road system versus areas without a road system, reflecting higher limits in remote regions.
- Alaska notes explaining that some home ownership median incomes apply only to RHS programs, not necessarily to HUD or LIHTC programs.
- Household-size adjustment rule for households larger than 8 persons.
- Arizona MSA and county-level limits for several metropolitan areas and counties, including Flagstaff, Phoenix, Tucson, Yuma, and others.
- A definition note for moderate income based on federal median income and area low-income formulas.

It lists income limits by county—specifically Wallace, Washington, Wichita, Wilson, and Woodson counties—for household sizes from 1 person through 8 persons. For each county, it provides thresholds for Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income (Guaranteed Loan) categories. The page also includes notes explaining that the 8-person limit increases by 8% of the 4-person limit for each additional person beyond 8, and that moderate income is defined using the greater of certain median-income-based calculations.

This partial document is a table from HUD’s Guaranteed Housing Program Income Limits for Kentucky, showing FY 2024 adjusted income limits by household size (1 to 8 persons) for different areas and metro regions.

Main points covered:
- It lists income limits for several Kentucky MSAs and HUD Metro FMR Areas, including:
  - Bowling Green area
  - Cincinnati, OH-KY-IN area
  - Clarksville, TN-KY area
  - Elizabethtown-Fort Knox area

  - Very Low Income
  - Low Income

- The limits are broken down by household size from 1 person up to 8 persons.

- Another note defines how “moderate income” is determined, based on comparisons to U.S. median family income, state median income, and area low-income limits.

This partial document is a table from HUD’s “Guaranteed Housing Program Income Limits” for Missouri, showing FY 2024 adjusted income limits by county and household size.

Main points covered:
- It lists Missouri counties and their income limits for housing program eligibility.
- For each county, it provides three categories:
  - Very Low Income
  - Low Income
  - Moderate Income – Guaranteed Loan
- Income limits are shown by household size from 1 person through 8 persons.
- The table includes footnotes explaining:
  - For households larger than 8, add 8% of the 4-person limit for each additional person.
  - The definition of moderate income is based on the greater of several median-income formulas.
- The excerpt includes counties such as Gasconade, Gentry, Grundy, Harrison, Henry, Hickory, Holt, Howell, Iron, Johnson, Knox, Laclede, Lawrence, Lewis, Linn, Livingston, Macon, and Madison.

It lists income thresholds by household size (1 to 8 persons) for several metropolitan and HUD metro areas, including Aguadilla-Isabela, Arecibo, Guayama, Mayagüez, Ponce, San Germán, and areas within the San Juan-Bayamón-Caguas region such as Barranquitas-Aibonito, Caguas, and Fajardo. For each area, the table provides limits for Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income Guaranteed Loan categories. It also includes notes explaining how income limits are adjusted for households larger than 8 persons and how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: San Juan-Bayamón-Caguas, PR MSA
nodes.summary: This partial document is a 2024 income limits table for HUD’s Guaranteed Housing Program in Puerto Rico. It lists adjusted income limits by household size (1 to 8 persons) for several metropolitan and metro-area regions, including Aguadilla-Isabela, Arecibo, Guayama, Mayagüez, Ponce, San German, and parts of the San Juan-Bayamón-Caguas area such as Barranquitas-Aibonito, Caguas, and Fajardo. For each area, the table gives thresholds for very low income, low income, and moderate income guaranteed loan eligibility. It also includes footnotes explaining how to calculate income limits for households larger than eight people and how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: Barranquitas-Aibonito, PR HUD Metro FMR Area
nodes.summary: This partial document is a USDA/HUD-style table from HB-1-3555 Appendix 5 listing FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the Guaranteed Housing Program in Puerto Rico. It provides income thresholds by household size (1 through 8 persons) for multiple areas and MSAs, including Aguadilla-Isabela, Arecibo, Guayama, Mayagüez, Ponce, San Germán, and parts of the San Juan-Bayamón-Caguas area such as Barranquitas-Aibonito, Caguas, and Fajardo. For each area, the table shows three program categories: Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income Guaranteed Loan limits. The footnotes explain how the 8-person limit is calculated and define moderate income as the greater of several income-based benchmarks. The page also includes the page number and revision date.

nodes.title: Caguas, PR HUD Metro FMR Area
nodes.summary: This partial document is a table from HUD’s HB-1-3555 Appendix 5 showing FY 2024 adjusted income limits for Puerto Rico under the Guaranteed Housing Program. It lists income thresholds by household size (1 to 8 persons) for several Puerto Rico metropolitan/statistical areas, including Aguadilla-Isabela, Arecibo, Guayama, Mayagüez, Ponce, San German, and areas within San Juan-Bayamón-Caguas such as Barranquitas-Aibonito, Caguas, and Fajardo. For each area, it provides categories for Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income Guaranteed Loan limits. The notes explain how limits are adjusted for households larger than eight persons and define how moderate income is calculated.

nodes.title: Fajardo, PR HUD Metro FMR Area
nodes.summary: The document is a FY 2024 HUD “Guaranteed Housing Program Income Limits” table for Puerto Rico. It lists adjusted income limits by household size (1 through 8 persons) for multiple Puerto Rico metropolitan and nonmetropolitan areas, including categories for Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income Guaranteed Loan eligibility. The partial pages cover several MSAs and HUD metro FMR areas such as Aguadilla-Isabela, Arecibo, Guayama, Mayagüez, Ponce, San Germán, San Juan–Bayamón–Caguas subareas, San Juan–Guaynabo, Yauco, and the Puerto Rico HUD Nonmetro Area. It also includes notes explaining how to calculate limits for households over 8 persons and how moderate income is defined, along with page and revision information.

nodes.title: San Juan-Guaynabo, PR HUD Metro FMR Area
nodes.summary: This partial document shows FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the USDA Guaranteed Housing Program in Puerto Rico. It lists income limits by household size (1 to 8 persons) for three areas: San Juan-Guaynabo HUD Metro FMR Area, Yauco MSA, and the Puerto Rico HUD Nonmetro Area. For each area, it provides thresholds for very low income, low income, and moderate income guaranteed loan eligibility. It also includes notes explaining how limits are adjusted for households larger than eight people and how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: Yauco, PR MSA
nodes.summary: This document page lists the FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the USDA Guaranteed Housing Program in Puerto Rico. It provides income thresholds by household size (1 through 8 persons) for three areas: San Juan-Guaynabo HUD Metro FMR Area, Yauco MSA, and the Puerto Rico HUD Nonmetro Area. For each area, it shows limits for Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income for guaranteed loans. The page also includes notes explaining how to calculate limits for households larger than eight people and how moderate income is defined, along with the page number and revision date.

nodes.title: Puerto Rico HUD Nonmetro Area
nodes.summary: This partial document shows FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the USDA Guaranteed Housing Program in Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands. It lists income thresholds by household size (1 to 8 persons) for three categories: Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income/Guaranteed Loan eligibility. The Puerto Rico section includes the San Juan-Guaynabo HUD Metro FMR Area, Yauco MSA, and the Puerto Rico HUD Nonmetro Area, with notes explaining how limits are adjusted for households larger than 8 and how moderate income is defined. The Virgin Islands section provides separate limits for St. Croix, St. John, and St. Thomas, along with the same footnotes and page/revision information.

nodes.title: STATE:VIRGIN ISLANDS
nodes.summary: This document section lists the FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the USDA Guaranteed Housing Program in the U.S. Virgin Islands. It provides income thresholds for different household sizes (1 to 8 persons) across three areas: St. Croix Island, St. John Island, and St. Thomas Island. For each island, it shows limits for Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income for Guaranteed Loans. The page also includes notes explaining how to calculate the 8-person household limit for families larger than eight and defines how moderate income is determined.

nodes.title: St. Croix Island, VI
nodes.summary: This partial document is a federal housing income limits table for the USDA Guaranteed Housing Program, showing FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the U.S. Virgin Islands. It lists income thresholds by household size (1 to 8 persons) for three regions: St. Croix Island, St. John Island, and St. Thomas Island. For each area, the table provides limits for Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income for guaranteed loans. It also includes a footnote explaining how to calculate limits for households larger than 8 persons and a definition of moderate income based on federal median income formulas.

nodes.title: St. John Island, VI
nodes.summary: The partial document is a HUD/FHA housing program income limits table for the Virgin Islands for FY 2024. It lists adjusted income limits by household size (1 to 8 persons) for three areas: St. Croix Island, St. John Island, and St. Thomas Island. For each island, it shows thresholds for Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income for Guaranteed Loans. The document also includes footnotes explaining how to calculate the 8-person limit and how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: St. Thomas Island, VI
nodes.summary: This document is an appendix table of Guaranteed Housing Program income limits for the U.S. Virgin Islands for FY 2024. It lists adjusted income limits by household size (1 through 8 persons) for three program categories—Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income Guaranteed Loan—for three locations: St. Croix Island, St. John Island, and St. Thomas Island. It also includes footnotes explaining how to calculate limits for households larger than 8 people and how moderate income is defined.
